portalsChecked: https://www.jobs.ac.uk (accountancy-and-finance PhD filter, business-management PhD filter, economics PhD filter, phd-scholarships category)
https://www.findaphd.com (homepage/category pages timed out or HTTP 403 to automated fetch)
Official .ac.uk pages: Henley/Reading, Liverpool ULMS, Manchester AMBS, Bristol UBBS, Durham, Surrey, Aston, UWE, Open University FBL, Warwick/MGS DTP, Leeds LUBS, Brunel EFA, Nottingham NUBS
searchSummary.note: As of 2026-07-12 the Sept-2026 England finance/accounting funded cycle is closed (typical deadlines Dec 2025–Jun 2026). jobs.ac.uk accountancy-and-finance PhD filter returns only Heriot-Watt Edinburgh (Scotland). No verified open funded pure finance/accounting studentships in England; two open UWE Bristol Business & Law studentships retained for compliance/governance fit. Jan/Sep 2027 funded finance rounds (MGS, AMBS, Henley, Liverpool GTF) not yet open — monitor from Oct 2026.

phdOffers.institution: UWE Bristol — Bristol Business School (College of Business and Law)
phdOffers.title: PhD Studentship — Water Governance Legal Frameworks and Regional Outcomes
phdOffers.subject: Comparative analysis of water-sector legal and regulatory architecture across England, Wales and Scotland amid privatisation and post-2025 integrated-regulator reforms; ownership, accountability, regional cost/benefit distribution, and climate adaptation governance.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: 3-year studentship from 1 Oct 2026: tax-exempt stipend £21,805 p.a. (2025/26 rate cited on page) plus full-time home/international tuition fees for up to three years.
phdOffers.notes: Reference 2627-OCT-CBL01 on application. Also on jobs.ac.uk (closes 9 Aug 2026 in portal vs 16 Aug on official page — use official). Requires 2:1+ and preferably Masters ≥65%; economics/environmental-law cognates accepted.

phdOffers.institution: UWE Bristol — Bristol Business School (College of Business and Law)
phdOffers.title: PhD Studentship — Sexual Harassment and Sexual Assault at Work: Legal and HR Blind Spots
phdOffers.subject: Mixed-methods study of how UK workplace sexual harassment/assault is routed through civil employment law and HR grievance processes rather than criminal justice; policy, compliance, and trauma-informed safeguarding implications for employers and unions.
phdOffers.fundingDetails: 3-year studentship from 1 Oct 2026: tax-exempt stipend £21,805 p.a. plus full-time home/international tuition fees for up to three years.
phdOffers.notes: Reference 2627-OCT-CBL02. Strong compliance/regulatory-policy angle for Dina; not core finance/accounting. Legal and/or HR qualifications expected.
